project.name: blackjack-server
project.description: Rust axum WebSocket server for multiplayer Blackjack with tokio async runtime, sqlx PostgreSQL persistence, and utoipa OpenAPI documentation.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Defines application configuration structs (Settings, ApplicationSettings, DatabaseSettings) and an Environment enum, loading layered config from YAML files and environment variables using the `config` crate.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Top-level settings struct aggregating ApplicationSettings and DatabaseSettings, with a `load` constructor that reads layered config files and environment variables.

nodes.name: DatabaseSettings
nodes.summary: Holds PostgreSQL connection parameters and exposes a `connection_string` method to build a sqlx-compatible connection URL.

nodes.name: Environment
nodes.summary: Enum representing deployment environment (Local/Production) with Display, as_str, and TryFrom<String> conversions for config file selection.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root that declares all sub-modules and defines the `App` struct — the shared application state holding connections counter, game session, wallet, and authenticator — plus the `AppState` type alias.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Arc<dyn Trait> for dependency injection of GameSession and Wallet, enabling testable swappable implementations.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Central application state struct holding an atomic connection counter, a dynamic game session, a dynamic wallet, and the in-memory authenticator, constructed via `new`.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Placeholder store module superseded by the session module; contains only a comment and no active code.

nodes.name: in_memory.rs
nodes.summary: In-memory implementation of the Wallet trait using a DashMap wrapped in Arc for concurrent per-player balance storage, supporting seed, balance, debit, credit, and set_balance operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses DashMap for lock-free concurrent access to balances; all operations are async via the Wallet trait.

nodes.name: InMemoryWallet
nodes.summary: Concrete Wallet implementation backed by an Arc<DashMap<PlayerId, u32>>, implementing balance queries, debit with insufficient-balance checking, credit, seed, and direct set_balance.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Wallet module root declaring the WalletError enum and the async Wallet trait defining balance, debit, credit, and set_balance operations over PlayerId.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses async_trait to enable async methods in a dyn-compatible trait; WalletError uses thiserror for ergonomic error formatting.

nodes.name: Wallet
nodes.summary: Async trait defining the wallet interface for balance queries and fund movements; Send + Sync ensures safe use behind Arc in multi-threaded axum handlers.

nodes.name: WalletError
nodes.summary: Error enum for wallet operations covering player-not-found and insufficient-balance cases with structured error message formatting via thiserror.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the WebSocket protocol message types for the Blackjack server: `ClientMessage` (all inbound player/dealer commands) and `ServerMessage` (all outbound server responses and events), serialized via serde.

nodes.name: ClientMessage
nodes.summary: Enum of all client-to-server WebSocket messages including Auth, JoinTable, PlaceBet, Hit, Stand, and dealer control actions.

nodes.name: ServerMessage
nodes.summary: Enum of all server-to-client WebSocket messages including AuthOk/AuthError, TableList, Snapshot, Event, CommandAck/CommandError, and Balance.

nodes.name: in_memory.rs
nodes.summary: Implements `InMemoryGameSession`, an in-process `GameSession` backend that manages a `DashMap` of live table actors, supports seeding tables with initial state, and routes player commands to table actors via mpsc channels.

nodes.name: InMemoryGameSession
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of the `GameSession` trait backed by an in-memory `DashMap`; spawns table actors on creation and exposes list, snapshot, send_command, and subscribe operations.

nodes.name: seed_table
nodes.summary: Creates a new table actor with initial game state, mpsc command channel, and broadcast event channel, then inserts a `TableHandle` into the session map.

nodes.name: list_tables
nodes.summary: Reads current `TableSummary` from each table's shared RwLock and returns a sorted list of all table summaries.

nodes.name: snapshot
nodes.summary: Sends a `TableCommand::Snapshot` to the target table actor via a oneshot channel and awaits the response.

nodes.name: send_command
nodes.summary: Sends a `TableCommand::Execute` with player action to the target table actor via a oneshot channel and awaits the acknowledgment result.

nodes.name: subscribe
nodes.summary: Returns a broadcast receiver for the specified table's `GameEvent` stream, allowing a WebSocket handler to receive live game events.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the session module's public interface: the `GameSession` async trait, `SessionError`, `RequestId`, and `CommandAck` types, and re-exports the sub-modules `in_memory`, `summary`, and `table_actor`.

nodes.name: GameSession
nodes.summary: Async trait defining the core session interface with `list_tables`, `snapshot`, `send_command`, and `subscribe` methods that all session backends must implement.

nodes.name: SessionError
nodes.summary: Error enum for session-level failures: `TableNotFound`, `CommandRejected`, and `Internal`.

nodes.name: summary.rs
nodes.summary: Defines `TableSummary`, a lightweight serializable struct representing the public state of a table (id, name, settings, player count, phase, joinability) used in lobby listings.

nodes.name: TableSummary
nodes.summary: Serializable snapshot of a table's public lobby-facing state including id, name, settings, player_count, phase, and is_joinable flag.

nodes.name: table_actor.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the `run_table_actor` tokio task: a command-processing loop that handles player and dealer commands, broadcasts game events, manages timer deadlines for betting/player-turn/round-delay, and drives automatic dealer progression.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `tokio::pin!` and `Box::pin` with `tokio::time::sleep` for async deadline timers; inner helper functions avoid borrow-checker issues by taking explicit mutable references to shared state.

nodes.name: TableCommand
nodes.summary: Enum of commands the table actor receives: `Execute` (player actions), `DealerExecute` (automatic dealer actions), and `Snapshot` (read-only state queries).

nodes.name: TableActorConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration struct for table actor timers: `betting_timeout`, `player_turn_timeout`, and `round_delay`, with sensible production defaults.

nodes.name: run_table_actor
nodes.summary: Public entry point for spawning a table actor task with default `TableActorConfig`; delegates immediately to `run_table_actor_with_config`.

nodes.name: run_table_actor_with_config
nodes.summary: Core table actor loop: selects over incoming `TableCommand` messages and timer deadlines, dispatches player/dealer actions through `GameEngine`, broadcasts events, updates lobby summary, and manages round/player-turn deadlines.

nodes.name: apply_and_broadcast
nodes.summary: Applies a list of game events to the current state and broadcasts each as a sequenced `GameEvent` on the broadcast channel.

nodes.name: update_summary
nodes.summary: Writes an updated `TableSummary` (phase string, player count, joinability) into the shared RwLock after each state transition.

nodes.name: handle_game_finished
nodes.summary: Settles player balances via the wallet and arms a round-delay deadline so the next round starts after a configurable pause.

nodes.name: fire_dealer
nodes.summary: Dispatches a single automatic dealer action through `GameEngine`, broadcasts the resulting events, updates the summary, and resets player or round timers as appropriate.

nodes.name: maybe_advance_dealer
nodes.summary: Checks the current game phase and, when appropriate, automatically fires the next dealer action (PlayHand or SettleRound) to drive the round to completion.

nodes.name: health.rs
nodes.summary: Implements a single GET /health endpoint that returns HTTP 200, used as a liveness probe for the server.

nodes.name: health_check
nodes.summary: Axum async handler for GET /health that immediately returns HTTP 200 OK to indicate service liveness.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Routes module barrel that assembles all HTTP and WebSocket routes into an axum Router, integrates utoipa OpenAPI documentation, and mounts a Redoc UI at /redoc.

nodes.name: openapi
nodes.summary: Returns the generated utoipa OpenAPI spec document by building the API router and extracting its OpenAPI component.

nodes.name: create_router
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the complete axum Router by composing all sub-routes, merging the Redoc documentation UI, and injecting AppState.

nodes.name: table.rs
nodes.summary: Provides the GET /tables HTTP endpoint that returns a JSON list of currently active blackjack table summaries from the session state.

nodes.name: list_tables
nodes.summary: Axum handler for GET /tables that queries the session actor for live table summaries and returns them as a JSON array.

nodes.name: ws.rs
nodes.summary: Core WebSocket handler implementing the full player connection lifecycle: authentication phase, lobby browsing, table join/leave with event forwarding, and all in-game player actions (bet, hit, stand, take/leave seat) over a persistent WebSocket connection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tokio mpsc channels and spawned tasks to forward server-sent game events to the WebSocket client concurrently; fwd_abort handles task cancellation when a player leaves a table.

nodes.name: ws_handler
nodes.summary: Axum HTTP handler for GET /ws that performs the WebSocket upgrade handshake and spawns handle_socket for the established connection.

nodes.name: handle_socket
nodes.summary: Manages the full lifecycle of a single WebSocket connection: tracks connection count, enforces an initial authentication phase, then drives the main message dispatch loop including graceful disconnect and automatic LeaveTable on close.

nodes.name: handle_client_msg
nodes.summary: Dispatches authenticated client messages to the appropriate session commands, handling JoinTable (with event-forwarding task), LeaveTable, Subscribe, Snapshot, and all player game actions (PlaceBet, Hit, Stand, TakeSeat, LeaveSeat).

nodes.name: send_player_cmd
nodes.summary: Sends a player action command to the session actor for a given table, then waits for the response and forwards the outcome (Ok or Err) back to the client via the WebSocket.

nodes.name: send_msg
nodes.summary: Serializes a ServerMessage to JSON and sends it as a WebSocket text frame, returning an error if serialization or send fails.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Project changelog tracking notable changes per release; documents the v0.1.0 milestone covering WebSocket server setup, GET /tables endpoint, config module, and initial commit.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the `server` crate (v0.1.0, edition 2021), declaring runtime dependencies including axum with WebSocket support, tokio, sqlx (PostgreSQL), serde, utoipa for OpenAPI generation, and a workspace-local dependency on `bj-core`.
nodes.languageNotes: Pulls `bj-core` as a local path dependency from the parent workspace, tying the server binary to the core game-logic crate.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Getting-started guide for the Blackjack WebSocket server covering requirements, how to run with `cargo run`, available HTTP/WebSocket endpoints, and RUST_LOG configuration.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yaml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose file that provisions a single PostgreSQL 16.1-alpine service with health checks, credential environment variables, and an init-scripts volume mount on a dedicated `blackjack` network.
nodes.languageNotes: Only the database service is defined here; the Rust application server is expected to run outside Docker (e.g., via `cargo run`), connecting to this container.

nodes.name: postgres
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 16.1-alpine container with health checks and credential injection via environment variables, used as the persistence layer for the blackjack server.

nodes.name: openapi.json
nodes.summary: OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification for the blackjack server, declaring three endpoints: GET /health (health check), GET /tables (list live tables), and GET /ws (WebSocket upgrade to 101).
nodes.languageNotes: Likely auto-generated by utoipa at build time rather than hand-authored; the `components` section is empty, suggesting schemas are inlined or not yet documented.

nodes.name: GET /health
nodes.summary: Health check endpoint returning HTTP 200 when the service is healthy.

nodes.name: GET /tables
nodes.summary: Lists all currently live blackjack tables, returning HTTP 200 with the table list.

nodes.name: GET /ws
nodes.summary: WebSocket upgrade endpoint responding with HTTP 101, establishing the persistent game connection.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion pattern file for the understand-anything analysis tool, listing glob patterns for files and directories to skip during codebase scanning.

nodes.name: base.yml
nodes.summary: Base application configuration defining default server port (3000) and PostgreSQL database connection settings including host, credentials, and connection pool size.

nodes.name: local.yml
nodes.summary: Local development configuration override that sets the application host to 127.0.0.1, layered on top of base.yml for local development.

nodes.name: load_test.rs
nodes.summary: In-process load test binary that spawns configurable numbers of concurrent player and observer tasks against live table actors, collecting round completion and command metrics for throughput analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tokio::spawn with Arc<AtomicU64> metrics counters and broadcast channels to coordinate concurrent players and observers without network overhead.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async entry point for the load test: parses CLI flags, creates table actors, spawns player and observer tasks, runs a live metrics ticker, then prints a final summary report.

nodes.name: player_task
nodes.summary: Async task simulating a single player: joins a table, places bets, waits for round events, and stands each hand in a loop until the deadline, incrementing round completion metrics.

nodes.name: create_table
nodes.summary: Constructs a full table actor in-process by initializing game state, channels, and a broadcast bus, then spawning the table actor task with configurable timers.

nodes.name: send_cmd
nodes.summary: Sends a command to a table actor over a channel and awaits a oneshot reply, tracking sent/error metrics atomically.

nodes.name: wait_for_event
nodes.summary: Receives broadcast events until a predicate matches or the deadline expires, used by player tasks to synchronize on game state transitions.

nodes.name: observer_task
nodes.summary: Async task that passively subscribes to table events and accumulates event-received and observer-lag metrics until the deadline.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Authentication module providing the Authenticator async trait and an InMemoryAuthenticator implementation that auto-creates accounts on first login and verifies passwords via the Password newtype.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses async_trait for dyn-safe async methods and RwLock<HashMap> for concurrent user store with a fast read-path and lazy signup write-path.

nodes.name: InMemoryAuthenticator
nodes.summary: Thread-safe in-memory user store implementing the Authenticator trait; auto-registers new users on first authenticate call and validates passwords for existing ones.

nodes.name: Authenticator
nodes.summary: Async trait defining the authentication contract: given an AuthPayload, return the PlayerId on success or an AuthError.

nodes.name: authenticate
nodes.summary: Async implementation of Authenticator for InMemoryAuthenticator: fast read-lock path for existing users with password check, falls through to write-lock signup for unknown usernames.

nodes.name: seed_user
nodes.summary: Pre-registers a username/password pair and returns the assigned PlayerId; idempotent — returns the existing PlayerId if the username is already registered.

nodes.name: password.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Password newtype wrapping secrecy::SecretString to prevent accidental logging or display of plaintext passwords, with constant-time equality and a redacted Debug impl.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the secrecy crate's SecretString to guarantee the inner value is zeroed on drop and never exposed through fmt::Debug.

nodes.name: Password
nodes.summary: Opaque password wrapper that holds a SecretString, exposes the value only via an explicit expose() call, and prints as [REDACTED] in Debug output.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Application entry point that loads configuration, seeds in-memory wallet and auth accounts, assembles AppState with session and router, then starts the axum TCP server.
nodes.languageNotes: Wires all service dependencies (wallet, auth, session) through Arc<dyn Trait> for runtime polymorphism before handing off to create_router.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async tokio main: initialises tracing, loads settings, seeds demo accounts, constructs AppState, and serves the axum router on the configured host/port.

nodes.name: openapi_schema.rs
nodes.summary: Golden-file integration test that serializes the live OpenAPI schema to JSON and compares it against the committed openapi.json; re-generates the golden file when BLESS=1 is set.

nodes.name: openapi_schema_is_up_to_date
nodes.summary: Test function that generates the current OpenAPI schema, optionally writes it as the new golden file (BLESS=1), or asserts it matches the committed snapshot.

layers.name: Application Entry Point
layers.description: Crate root and binary entry point that wire together all modules, define shared AppState, load configuration, seed runtime data, and start the axum TCP server.

layers.name: HTTP & WebSocket API Layer
layers.description: Axum route handlers and router assembly covering the health probe, table listing, and the full WebSocket player connection lifecycle including authentication, lobby, and in-game actions.

layers.name: Game Engine
layers.description: Core blackjack game logic implemented as tokio actor tasks managing table state, command dispatch, event broadcasting, timer deadlines, and in-memory session registry.

layers.name: Domain Services
layers.description: Cross-cutting domain modules providing player authentication, password security, wallet balance management, WebSocket protocol message type definitions, and application configuration structs.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Docker Compose service definitions for the PostgreSQL dependency, YAML application configuration for server and database settings, Cargo build manifest, and the OpenAPI 3.1 specification with declared endpoints.

layers.name: Testing & Documentation
layers.description: Integration and load tests validating OpenAPI schema correctness and actor concurrency under load, alongside project documentation and tooling exclusion rules.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README introduces the Blackjack WebSocket server: a multiplayer Blackjack game backend built with Rust, axum, and tokio. It covers the three main endpoints (WebSocket, /health, /tables), how to run the server, and how to configure log verbosity via RUST_LOG. Reading this first gives you the mental model — a persistent WebSocket connection per player, over which the entire game lifecycle flows.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: main.rs wires the entire runtime together: it loads layered YAML configuration, seeds in-memory wallet balances and authentication accounts for test players, constructs the shared AppState, assembles the axum Router, and binds the TCP listener. This is the 'big bang' of the server — everything else is either assembled here or called from something assembled here.
tour.languageLesson: Rust binaries use a `#[tokio::main]` attribute macro to turn an async fn main into a synchronous entry point that sets up and drives the tokio runtime. The macro expands to a call to `tokio::runtime::Runtime::block_on`, keeping the binary startup simple.

tour.title: Shared Application State
tour.description: lib.rs is the crate root and defines the central App struct — the single piece of shared state threaded through every request handler. It holds an atomic connections counter, the GameSession (table management), the Wallet (player balances), and the Authenticator. The AppState type alias wraps App in Arc so axum can clone it cheaply across tasks. Understanding this struct is essential because every handler receives it via axum's State extractor.
tour.languageLesson: Wrapping shared state in `Arc<T>` (Atomically Reference Counted) allows multiple tokio tasks to hold a reference without copying. Because axum handlers run in separate tasks, State<AppState> clones the Arc on each request — O(1) cost, no heap allocation for the inner data.

tour.title: Configuration System
tour.description: config.rs and its companion base.yml together implement a two-layer configuration system. The Rust structs (Settings, ApplicationSettings, DatabaseSettings) define the expected shape, while base.yml provides default values for server port and PostgreSQL connection. An Environment enum allows the loader to overlay environment-specific files and environment variables on top of the base, making the server configurable without recompilation.
tour.languageLesson: The `config` crate reads layered YAML files in order (base → environment → env vars). Rust's serde `Deserialize` derive macro handles the JSON/YAML-to-struct mapping. The Environment enum's `as_str` method converts enum variants to file-name suffixes (e.g., `local` → `configuration/local.yml`).

tour.title: WebSocket Protocol Contract
tour.description: protocol/mod.rs defines the complete message vocabulary shared between client and server. ClientMessage enumerates every action a player can take — authenticate, join table, place bet, hit, stand, double-down — while ServerMessage enumerates every event the server can push back — deal results, game state snapshots, errors, lobby updates. This file is the specification for the WebSocket wire format; reading it tells you everything the game can do without reading a single handler.
tour.languageLesson: Rust enums with `#[serde(tag = "type")]` produce tagged JSON unions. Each variant serializes to `{"type": "Hit", ...fields}`, and deserialization dispatches on the `type` field. This is more type-safe than a generic message-plus-payload pattern because the compiler guarantees exhaustive matching over all variants.

tour.title: Route Assembly & OpenAPI
tour.description: routes/mod.rs is the routing barrel: it composes all axum route handlers into a single Router and integrates utoipa to auto-generate an OpenAPI 3.1 spec from the handler annotations. It also mounts a Redoc UI at /redoc, giving developers a self-documenting API explorer in the running server. This is where HTTP-level concerns (paths, methods, middleware) are centralized, separate from the handler logic.

tour.title: WebSocket Connection Lifecycle
tour.description: routes/ws.rs is the heart of the player experience. It handles the WebSocket upgrade and then drives the player through three phases: authentication (verify credentials via the Authenticator), lobby browsing (list tables, join or create), and in-game actions (bet, hit, stand, double). Each incoming ClientMessage from the protocol layer is matched and dispatched to the appropriate GameSession method. The handler also manages graceful disconnection, cleaning up table seats when a WebSocket closes.
tour.languageLesson: axum's `WebSocketUpgrade` extractor returns a response immediately and hands a `WebSocket` handle to a callback. The callback owns the connection and runs as an independent tokio task — this is how one server handles thousands of concurrent players without blocking threads.

tour.title: HTTP Endpoints: Health & Tables
tour.description: health.rs and table.rs provide the two plain HTTP endpoints. The health endpoint returns 200 OK for liveness probes. The tables endpoint queries the GameSession for all live TableSummary records and returns them as JSON, powering the lobby view in any client UI. These handlers are thin by design — they delegate all logic to the session layer and contain no game state themselves.

tour.title: Session Trait: Game Backend Abstraction
tour.description: session/mod.rs defines the GameSession async trait — the contract that separates game business logic from its storage backend. The trait declares operations like create_table, join_table, send_command, and list_tables. SessionError, RequestId, and CommandAck provide the error and identity types used across the session boundary. This abstraction means the WebSocket handler never knows whether tables are stored in memory or in a database.
tour.languageLesson: Rust async traits require the `async_trait` crate (or Rust 1.75+ native async fn in trait) because async fns produce impl Future types that vary per implementation, which the trait object system cannot handle directly. The macro rewrites each async fn into a fn returning `Pin<Box<dyn Future>>`.

tour.title: In-Memory Session & Table Actor
tour.description: in_memory.rs implements GameSession using a DashMap of table IDs to running tokio task handles. When a table is created it spawns a run_table_actor task; when it is closed the task is shut down. table_actor.rs contains the actual game loop: it processes commands through a channel, evaluates game rules (deal, hit, stand, bust, blackjack), broadcasts ServerMessage events to all connected players, and manages countdown timers for automatic dealer progression. Together these two files are the entire game engine.
tour.languageLesson: The actor pattern in async Rust isolates mutable state inside a single tokio task. External code sends commands through an `mpsc` channel and receives results via a `oneshot` reply channel embedded in the command. This avoids Mutex contention — the actor task is the only writer, so there is no lock.

tour.title: Table Lobby Summary
tour.description: session/summary.rs defines the TableSummary struct: a small, serializable snapshot of a table's public state (table ID, player count, current phase, minimum bet). This lightweight view is what the /tables HTTP endpoint and lobby WebSocket messages return to clients. Keeping it separate from the full internal table state ensures the lobby is always fast to query without exposing game secrets like hole cards.

tour.title: Authentication & Password Safety
tour.description: auth/mod.rs provides the Authenticator async trait and InMemoryAuthenticator, which holds a map of username to hashed credentials. auth/password.rs defines the Password newtype over secrecy::SecretString — a wrapper that prevents the plaintext password from ever appearing in logs or Debug output by implementing a custom Display and Debug that print only '[REDACTED]'. This is the WebSocket handler's first gate: no authentication, no game access.
tour.languageLesson: The newtype pattern in Rust wraps a primitive in a single-field struct to give it a distinct type and controlled API. `secrecy::SecretString` goes further by zeroing memory on drop (zeroize) and refusing to implement Display, making accidental secret exposure a compile-time error rather than a runtime surprise.

tour.title: Wallet: Player Balances
tour.description: wallet/mod.rs declares the async Wallet trait with balance, debit, credit, and set_balance operations, plus WalletError variants for insufficient funds and unknown players. wallet/in_memory.rs implements it with a DashMap wrapped in Arc, providing lock-free concurrent reads and fine-grained shard-level locking for writes. The Wallet is seeded at startup in main.rs and debited when bets are placed, credited when players win.
tour.languageLesson: DashMap achieves concurrent map access without a single global Mutex by internally sharding entries and locking only the relevant shard. For a wallet where many players read balances simultaneously but writes are rare, this is significantly more scalable than `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>`.

tour.title: OpenAPI Spec & Infrastructure
tour.description: openapi.json is the committed golden-file snapshot of the server's OpenAPI 3.1 specification, covering all three endpoints. docker-compose.yaml provisions the PostgreSQL 16.1-alpine database dependency with a health check, enabling a one-command local development environment. Together these files represent the API contract and the infrastructure contract: what the server exposes and what it depends on.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose `depends_on` with `condition: service_healthy` ensures the app container only starts once PostgreSQL passes its health check (`pg_isready`). This prevents connection-refused errors during startup that would otherwise require retry logic in the application code.

tour.title: Testing & Load Validation
tour.description: tests/openapi_schema.rs is a golden-file integration test: it serializes the live OpenAPI schema from utoipa and diffs it against the committed openapi.json, catching any unintentional API contract drift in CI. examples/load_test.rs is an in-process load test that spawns configurable numbers of concurrent player and observer tasks against a running server, validating that the actor-per-table design holds up under parallel traffic. Both are essential quality gates for a WebSocket server where correctness and concurrency interact.
